text: An innovative aspect of our approach is that each edge is associated with additional information beyond its dependency label. This information is structured as follows: SRC: a field indicating the origin of this edgeeither "UD" for the original dependency edges, or a pair indicating the type and sub-type of the construction that resulted in the BART edge (e.g., {SRC=(conj,and)} or {SRC=(adv,while)}). UNC, ALT: optional fields indicating uncertainty, described below.

metadata.title: pyBART: Evidence-based Syntactic Transformations for IE

text: “Colours for the counties,” said Wilkins, “and if there’s no coloured cloth to be had, there’s always red flannel. Anything is preferable to leaving the crowd of people to go roaming about....”
source_text: "Colours for the counties," said Wilkins, "and if there isn't coloured cloth to be got there's--red flannel. Anything is better than leaving the mass of people to mob about...."

text: “It is of no importance,” I said, accompanying the words with a gesture of the most perfect indifference; “give me the grand total, please.”
source_text: “It is of no consequence,” I said, accompanying the words with a gesture of the most utter indifference; “give me the grand total, please.”

text: The trimmings of her dress, trembling in the light, betrayed how deeply she was moved, and at last she broke into tears. “And you will not—urge me—upon anything further—if I say in five or six years?” she sobbed, when she could at length shape the words.
source_text: The trimmings of her dress, as they quivered against the light, showed how agitated she was, and at last she burst out crying. “And you’ll not—press me—about anything more—if I say in five or six years?” she sobbed, when she had power to frame the words.

text:
An anguish at the naming,
That, when to patience grown,
I’ve known leave then granted
To join itself to its own.
source_text:
An anguish at the mention,
Which, when to patience grown,
I 've known permission given
To rejoin its own.

text:
For he hears the lambs’ innocent call,
And he hears the ewes’ tender reply;
He is watchful while they are in peace,
For they know when their shepherd is nigh.

metadata.title: Songs of Innocence and of Experience

text: To identify which explanations matter most, we organize them into several semantic categories (see Appendix A.3) and then add them cumulatively to the NoExp baseline. In particular, for Spouse we divide the explanations into the groups MARRIED (10 explanations), CHILDREN (5), ENGAGED (3), NEGA-TIVES (13), and MISC (9). Figure 3 shows that ExpBERT matches the performance of the NoExp baseline with 20x less data on Spouse (Left) and with 3x less data on TACRED (Right). We find that adding additional explanation groups improves performance (Table 3), which suggests that wide coverage of different explanatory factors may be beneficial for performance. We also observe that the MARRIED group, which contains paraphrases of {o 1 } is married to {o 2 }, by itself improves over NoExp, suggesting that multiple paraphrases of the same explanation can improve performance.
source_text: To understand which explanations are important, we group explanations into a few semantic categories (details in Appendix A.3) and cumulatively add them to the NoExp baseline. In particular, we break down explanations for Spouse into the Figure 3 : ExpBERT matches the performance of the NoExp baseline with 20x less data on Spouse (Left), and with 3x less data on TACRED (Right). groups MARRIED (10 explanations), CHILDREN (5 explanations), ENGAGED (3 explanations), NEGA-TIVES (13 explanations) and MISC (9 explanations). We find that adding new explanation groups helps performance (Table 3) , which suggests that a broad coverage of various explanatory factors could be helpful for performance. We also observe that the MARRIED group (which contains paraphrases of {o 1 } is married to {o 2 }) alone boosts performance over NoExp, which suggests that a variety of paraphrases of the same explanation can improve performance.

text:
From imperfection’s murkiest cloud,
Darts always forth one ray of perfect light,
One flash of heaven’s glory.
He waited and waited, and the clock struck twelve; yet Doctor Manette came not back. Mr. Lorry returned, and found no tidings of him, nor brought any. Where could he be?
source_text: He waited and waited, and the clock struck twelve; but Doctor Manette did not come back. Mr. Lorry returned, and found no tidings of him, and brought none. Where could he be?

text: “And you must!” she said, with gratitude. “A thousand thanks for your devotion, Gabriel! Good night—I know you are doing all you can for me.”
source_text: “And you better!” she said, gratefully. “Thank you for your devotion, a thousand times, Gabriel! Goodnight—I know you are doing your very best for me.”

text:
Behold, through thee, as base as all the rest,
Through laughter, dancing, feasting, supping, of the crowd,
Within the gowns and trinkets, within those wash’d and tended faces,
Behold a hidden, voiceless loathing and despair.
source_text:
Behold through you as bad as the rest,
Through the laughter, dancing, dining, supping, of people,
Inside of dresses and ornaments, inside of those wash’d and trimm’d faces,
Behold a secret silent loathing and despair.

text:
I sing to fill the waiting,
My bonnet to just tie,
And close the door to my little house;
No more is left to try,
source_text:
I sing to use the waiting,
My bonnet but to tie,
And shut the door unto my house;
No more to do have I,

text: Because ConvAI2 was a competition, many participants developed strong models for it. The strongest results were achieved by large pre-trained transformers (Dinan et al., 2020). In particular, Wolf et al. (2019b) used pretraining following the approach of Radford et al. (2018) on the BooksCorpus dataset, which yielded the best perplexity and F1 scores. More recently, performance has improved further with more effective and larger-scale pretraining (Lewis et al., 2019), which we compare against here; that study also reports strong results on ELI5.
source_text: As ConvAI2 was a competition, a number of competitors built strong models on it. The best results were obtained by large pre-trained transformers (Dinan et al., 2020) . In particular, Wolf et al. (2019b) pre-trained via the method of Radford et al. (2018) using the BooksCorpus dataset, resulting in the best perplexities and F1 scores. Since then, results have gotten even better with the advent of better and larger pretraining (Lewis et al., 2019 ), which we compare to here; the same work also reports strong results on ELI5.

text: “Take it easy, Sandy. All I need to know is how _much_ of an ogre is invisible; then I’ll know where to find his vitals. Don’t be frightened—I’ll make short work of these bunco-steerers. Stay right where you are.”
source_text: “You be easy, Sandy. All I need to know is, how _much_ of an ogre is invisible; then I know how to locate his vitals. Don't you be afraid, I will make short work of these bunco-steerers. Stay where you are.”

text: “And wot,” said Sikes, scowling fiercely at his agreeable friend, “wot makes you put yourself to so much trouble over one chalk-faced kid, when you know there are fifty boys snoozing about Common Garden every night, from among which you might pick and choose?”
source_text: “And wot,” said Sikes, scowling fiercely on his agreeable friend, “wot makes you take so much pains about one chalk-faced kid, when you know there are fifty boys snoozing about Common Garden every night, as you might pick and choose from?”

text: “But then, may not I be peculiarly constituted to write?” he queried, secretly exulting at the language he had used, his swift imagination throwing the whole scene and atmosphere upon a vast screen along with a thousand other scenes from his life—scenes that were rough and raw, gross and bestial.

Then come home, my children, the sun has gone down,  
And the night dews are rising high;  
Come, come, put aside your play, and let us be away,  
Till the morning opens in the sky.
source_text:
‘Then come home, my children, the sun is gone down,
And the dews of night arise;
Come, come, leave off play, and let us away,
Till the morning appears in the skies.’

text: “You are ambitious, Eustacia—no, not quite ambitious, but luxurious. I ought, I suppose, to be of the same temper, if I am to make you happy. And yet, so far from it, I could live and die in a hermitage here, provided only that I had honest work to occupy me.”
source_text: “You are ambitious, Eustacia—no, not exactly ambitious, luxurious. I ought to be of the same vein, to make you happy, I suppose. And yet, far from that, I could live and die in a hermitage here, with proper work to do.”

text: Every conversation of the cottagers now revealed fresh marvels to me. As I listened to the lessons Felix imparted to the Arabian, the strange order of human society was gradually made known to me. I heard of the division of property, of vast riches and miserable poverty, of rank, ancestry, and noble blood.
source_text: “Every conversation of the cottagers now opened new wonders to me. While I listened to the instructions which Felix bestowed upon the Arabian, the strange system of human society was explained to me. I heard of the division of property, of immense wealth and squalid poverty, of rank, descent, and noble blood.

text:
Oft as beside the winding Nith I wait,  
At sober eve, or hail the gladsome dawn,  
I’ll miss thee sporting o’er the dewy lawn,  
And curse the ruffian’s aim, and mourn thy hapless fate.
source_text:
Oft as by winding Nith I, musing, wait
The sober eve, or hail the cheerful dawn,
I’ll miss thee sporting o’er the dewy lawn,
And curse the ruffian’s aim, and mourn thy hapless fate.

text: Fairway, meanwhile, had finished a searching look at Yeobright. “Now, you may not believe it,” he said to the others in the room, “but I should never have knowed this gentleman if I’d come across him anywhere away from his own he’th—he’s changed so greatly.”
source_text: Fairway had meanwhile concluded a critical gaze at Yeobright. “Now, you may not believe it,” he said to the rest of the room, “but I should never have knowed this gentleman if I had met him anywhere off his own he’th—he’s altered so much.”

text:
The hillsides must not know it,
Where I have roamed so far,
Nor whisper to the loving woods
The day that I depart,
source_text:
The hillsides must not know it,
Where I have rambled so,
Nor tell the loving forests
The day that I shall go,

text:
As down the stream they made their way,  
And through the blooming vale;  
His cheek to hers he oft would lay,  
And love was still the tale:
source_text:
As down the burn they took their way,
And thro’ the flowery dale;
His cheek to hers he aft did lay,
And love was aye the tale:

text: “Oh yes,” she said hastily. “I know all that. But do not speak of it—seven years, or even six—where may we all be by that time?”
source_text: “Oh yes,” she said, quickly. “I know all that. But don’t talk of it—seven or six years—where may we all be by that time?”

text:
To know him wholly, we must read his verse  
with letters set beside it, as a gloss  
that shows in his dear nature humour’s grace,  
clear, piercing wisdom, frankness, strength, and loss  
of none of sympathy and tolerant art.  
So may we enter mind and beating heart  
of that friend who, speaking for so many, said  
of Keats one whom he did not, and does not,  
more fully praise in genius than he loved  
the man entire.
source_text:
Fully to understand him we must read his poetry with the commentary of
his letters which reveal in his character elements of humour,
clear-sighted wisdom, frankness, strength, sympathy and tolerance. So
doing we shall enter into the mind and heart of the friend who, speaking
for many, described Keats as one 'whose genius I did not, and do not,
more fully admire than I entirely loved the man'.

text: Clym set his breakfast aside. “Then there is some kind of scandal, and that is what is troubling Thomasin. Was this what made her ill?”
source_text: Clym pushed away his breakfast. “Then there is a scandal of some sort, and that’s what’s the matter with Thomasin. Was it this that made her ill?”

text: Hand in hand they went along the path toward Mistover. When they were close to the house, he said, “It is too late for me to see your grandfather tonight. Do you think he will object?”
source_text: Hand in hand they went along the path towards Mistover. When they were near the house he said, “It is too late for me to see your grandfather tonight. Do you think he will object to it?”

text: “It was quite a mistake,” Bathsheba went on, “for aunt to tell you I had a young man already. I have no sweetheart at all—and never have had one; and I thought that, as things go with women, it was such a pity to let you go away believing I had several.”
source_text: “—It was quite a mistake—aunt’s telling you I had a young man already,” Bathsheba went on. “I haven’t a sweetheart at all—and I never had one, and I thought that, as times go with women, it was _such_ a pity to send you away thinking that I had several.”

text: Dempsey nodded at Andy and William McMahan, the secretary of the club, and walked rapidly toward a door at the rear of the hall. Two other members of the Give and Take Association swiftly joined the little group. Terry O’Sullivan was now in the hands of the Board of Rules and Social Referees. They spoke to him briefly and softly, and conducted him out through the same door at the rear.
“But think of him now!” she exclaimed with fervor. “Think what his income makes possible for him. His early privations have been repaid a thousand times over.”
source_text: “But think of him now!” she cried enthusiastically. “Think of what his income affords him. His early denials are paid for a thousand-fold.”

text: We train and evaluate our model on the PARANMT-50M paraphrase dataset, which was constructed by back-translating the Czech sentences in the CzEng corpus (Bojar et al., 2016). We further filter this dataset to exclude shorter sentences (fewer than 8 tokens), low-quality paraphrase pairs, as measured by a translation score provided with the dataset, and examples showing little reordering, as quantified by a reordering score based on the position of each word in the source sentence and its aligned word in the target sentence. After filtering, the dataset contains over 350k paired paraphrases.
source_text: We train and evaluate our model on the PARANMT-50M paraphrase dataset constructed by backtranslating the Czech sentences of the CzEng (Bojar et al., 2016) corpus. We filter this dataset to remove shorter sentences (less than 8 tokens), low quality paraphrase pairs (quantified by a translation score included with the dataset) and examples that exhibit low reordering (quantified by a reordering score based on the position of each word in the source and its aligned word in the target sentence). This leaves us with over 350k paired paraphrase pairs.

text: “My life is a burden apart from you,” he cried in a low voice. “I want you—I want you to let me tell you, again and again, that I love you!”
source_text: “My life is a burden without you,” he exclaimed, in a low voice. “I want you—I want you to let me say I love you again and again!”

text:
An ancient tower to memory brought
How Dettingen’s bold hero fought;
Still, far from sinking into nought,
It owns a lord
Who far in western climates fought,
With trusty sword.

metadata.title: Poems and Songs of Robert Burns

text:
While guileless neighbors
Converse of the "early dead,"
We, prone to circumlocution,
Observe that birds are fled!
source_text:
While simple-hearted neighbors
Chat of the 'early dead,'
We, prone to periphrasis,
Remark that birds have fled!

text: “It is life,” he said, “and life is not always beautiful. And yet, perhaps because I am oddly made, I find something beautiful in it. It seems to me that the beauty is made ten times greater because it is there—”
source_text: “It is life,” he said, “and life is not always beautiful. And yet, perhaps because I am strangely made, I find something beautiful there. It seems to me that the beauty is tenfold enhanced because it is there—”

text:
‘I have no name;
I am only two days old.’
What shall I call you?
‘I am full of joy,
Joy is my name.’
May sweet joy be yours!
source_text:
‘I have no name;
I am but two days old.’
What shall I call thee?
‘I happy am,
Joy is my name.’
Sweet joy befall thee!

text: At this moment, the Artful one, affecting an exactness in readiness for what might follow, requested that the turnkey would be pleased to acquaint him with “the names of them two chaps as was sittin’ on the bench.” This so delighted the company, that they laughed nearly as freely as young Tom might have done had the question reached his ears.
source_text: At this point, the Dodger, with a show of being very particular with a view to proceedings to be had thereafter, desired the jailer to communicate “the names of them two files as was on the bench.” Which so tickled the spectators, that they laughed almost as heartily as Master Bates could have done if he had heard the request.

text: “Were you dancing with her, Diggory?” she asked, in a tone that showed he had, by this revelation, made himself a good deal more interesting to her.
source_text: “Were you dancing with her, Diggory?” she asked, in a voice which revealed that he had made himself considerably more interesting to her by this disclosure.

text: “As I am sure the men were at the window,” replied Oliver, pointing, as he spoke, to the hedge that separated the cottage garden from the meadow. “The tall man sprang over there; and the Jew, after running a few paces to the right, slipped through that opening.”
source_text: “As I am that the men were at the window,” replied Oliver, pointing down, as he spoke, to the hedge which divided the cottage-garden from the meadow. “The tall man leaped over, just there; and the Jew, running a few paces to the right, crept through that gap.”

text:
Now here, and these, and hence in peace, all yours, O Flag!  
And here, and hence for you, O universal Muse—and you for them!  
And here, and hence, O Union, all the labor and laborers yours!  
None parted from you—henceforth one alone, we and you,  
(For the blood of children, what is it but the mother’s blood?  
And lives and works—what are they in the end but paths to faith and death?)
source_text:
Now here and these and hence in peace, all thine O Flag!
And here and hence for thee, O universal Muse! and thou for them!
And here and hence O Union, all the work and workmen thine!
None separate from thee--henceforth One only, we and thou,
(For the blood of the children, what is it, only the blood maternal?
And lives and works, what are they all at last, except the roads to
faith and death?)

text: “In a word,” Madame Defarge went on, “my husband has not my reason for pursuing this family to annihilation, and I have not his reason for regarding this Doctor with any sensibility. I must act for myself, therefore. Come hither, little citizen.”

CHORUS OF SPIRITS AND HOURS:
Then weave the web of the mystic measure;
From the depths of the sky and the ends of the earth,
Come, swift Spirits of might and of pleasure,
Fill the dance and the music of mirth,
As the waves of a thousand streams rush by
To an ocean of splendour and harmony!

metadata.title: The Complete Poetical Works of Percy Bysshe Shelley — Complete

text: Compared with two strong baselines, BERT and RoBERTa 7, SMART, including SMART BERT and SMART RoBERTa, consistently surpasses both across all 8 GLUE tasks by a large margin. When evaluated against BERT, SMART BERT reached 85.6% (vs. 84.5%) and 86.0% (vs. 84.4%) in accuracy, representing absolute gains of 1.1% and 1.6% on the MNLI in-domain and out-of-domain settings. (Liu et al., 2019c) 90.2/-92.2/-86.6 94.7 -/90.9 68.0 96.4 92.4/-PGD (Zhu et al., 2020) 90.5/-92.5/-87.4 94.9 -/90.9 69.7 96.4 92.4/-FreeAT (Zhu et al., 2020) 90.0/-92.5/-86.7 94.7 -/90.7 68.8 96.1 92.4/-FreeLB (Zhu et al., 2020) 90 Table 2: GLUE test set results scored using the GLUE evaluation server. The state-of-the-art results are shown in bold. . * ALBERT uses a model similar in size, architecture and computation cost to a 3,000M BERT (though it has dramatically fewer parameters due to parameter sharing). † Mixed results from ensemble and single of MT-DNN-SMART and with data augmentation.
source_text: Comparing with two strong baselines BERT and RoBERTa 7 , SMART, including SMART BERT and SMART RoBERTa , consistently outperforms them across all 8 GLUE tasks by a big margin. Comparing with BERT, SMART BERT obtained 85.6% (vs. 84.5%) and 86.0% (vs. 84.4%) in terms of accuracy, which is 1.1% and 1.6% absolute improvement, on the MNLI in-domain and out-domain settings. (Liu et al., 2019c) 90.2/-92.2/-86.6 94.7 -/90.9 68.0 96.4 92.4/-PGD (Zhu et al., 2020) 90.5/-92.5/-87.4 94.9 -/90.9 69.7 96.4 92.4/-FreeAT (Zhu et al., 2020) 90.0/-92.5/-86.7 94.7 -/90.7 68.8 96.1 92.4/-FreeLB (Zhu et al., 2020) 90 Table 2 : GLUE test set results scored using the GLUE evaluation server. The state-of-the-art results are in bold. . * ALBERT uses a model similar in size, architecture and computation cost to a 3,000M BERT (though it has dramatically fewer parameters due to parameter sharing). † Mixed results from ensemble and single of MT-DNN-SMART and with data augmentation.

text:
A sight of joy and marvel to behold  
That river’s forms and shadows ever changing,  
Where the wide dawn brimmed over with deep gold  
Its whirling pools, where every tint was ranging;  
And where sweet falls in song and spray were ranging  
Among rocks garlanded with flowers, the foam and spray  
Shone like bright stars upon the sunlit river,  
Or when the moonlight shed a holier day,  
One broad, bright lake lay round green islands, glittering gray.
source_text:
A scene of joy and wonder to behold
That river’s shapes and shadows changing ever,
Where the broad sunrise filled with deepening gold
Its whirlpools, where all hues did spread and quiver;
And where melodious falls did burst and shiver
Among rocks clad with flowers, the foam and spray
Sparkled like stars upon the sunny river,
Or when the moonlight poured a holier day,
One vast and glittering lake around green islands lay.

text:
Words—book-words—what are you?
No words at all, for listen and look:
My song is out there in the open air, and I must sing,
With banner and pennant fluttering.
source_text:
Words! book-words! what are you?
Words no more, for hearken and see,
My song is there in the open air, and I must sing,
With the banner and pennant a-flapping.

text:
Men of England, wherefore plough
For the lords who lay ye low?
Wherefore weave with toil and care
The rich robes your tyrants wear?

metadata.title: The Complete Poetical Works of Percy Bysshe Shelley — Complete

text: “There is cause for such ghastliness. Eustacia, you have held my happiness in the hollow of your hand, and, like a devil, you have flung it to the ground!”
source_text: “There’s reason for ghastliness. Eustacia, you have held my happiness in the hollow of your hand, and like a devil you have dashed it down!”

text:
‘Time passed—I know not whether months or years;
For day, nor night, nor turn of seasons made
Its mark, but thoughts and tears that nothing cheers:
And I grew, in the end, as one half-shade,
A smoke, a cloud the winds have worn and frayed,
Till it was thin as air; until, one eve,
A Nautilus upon the fountain strayed,
Unfurling his blue sail where Heaven’s breath did not leave,
Among the waves and whirling eddies driven.
source_text:
‘Time passed, I know not whether months or years;
For day, nor night, nor change of seasons made
Its note, but thoughts and unavailing tears:
And I became at last even as a shade,
A smoke, a cloud on which the winds have preyed,
Till it be thin as air; until, one even,
A Nautilus upon the fountain played,
Spreading his azure sail where breath of Heaven
Descended not, among the waves and whirlpools driven.

text: “I entreat you to observe that I have come hither of my own free will, in answer to that written appeal of a fellow-countryman which lies before you. I ask no more than the opportunity to do so without delay. Is not that my right?”
source_text: “I entreat you to observe that I have come here voluntarily, in response to that written appeal of a fellow-countryman which lies before you. I demand no more than the opportunity to do so without delay. Is not that my right?”

text: The dinner was concluded, the waiter generously fed, and the two walked out to the corner where they had met. Miss Marian walked very well now; her limp was scarcely noticeable.

Natural language explanations can capture different types of inductive biases and prior knowledge, but some types of prior knowledge are of course better introduced through other means. We wrap up our experiments with a vignette on how language explanations can complement other forms of feature and representation engineering. We consider Disease, where we have access to an external ontology (Comparative Toxicogenomic Database or CTD) from Wei et al. (2015) containing chemicaldisease interactions. Following Hancock et al. (2018), we add 6 bits to the explanation representation v(x) that test if the given chemical-disease pair follows certain relations in CTD (e.g., if they are in the ctd-therapy dictionary). Table 5 shows that as expected, other sources of information can complement language explanations in ExpBERT.

metadata.title: ExpBERT: Representation Engineering with Natural Language Explanations
metadata.section: Complementing language explanations with external databases

text: “I am not here by chance,” he added, after a long silence; “nor have I learned all this tonight, for I knew it yesterday—yesterday only. Do you think that I have come to remind you of a promise?”
source_text: “I am not here by accident,” he added after a lengthened silence; “nor have I heard all this tonight, for I knew it yesterday—only yesterday. Do you guess that I have come to remind you of a promise?”

text: “And he shall have it, too!” cried the man, dealing him yet another blow and catching Oliver by the collar. “Come on, you young villain! Here, Bull’s-eye, look to him, boy! Look to him!”
source_text: “And he shall have it, too!” rejoined the man, administering another blow, and seizing Oliver by the collar. “Come on, you young villain! Here, Bull’s-eye, mind him, boy! Mind him!”

text: “It’s a damned hard business, I know,” the other went on, “but I sort of hate to see you fall off the wagon, Mart. Well, here goes!”
source_text: “It’s fair hell, I know,” the other went on, “but I kind of hate to see you come off the wagon, Mart. Well, here’s how!”

text: “There’s the black suit,” the pawnbroker, who knew every article of his belongings, had answered. “You needn’t tell me you’ve gone and pledged it with that Jew, Lipka. For if you have—”
source_text: “There’s the black suit,” the pawnbroker, who knew his every asset, had answered. “You needn’t tell me you’ve gone and pledged it with that Jew, Lipka. Because if you have—”

text:
The Black man grips the reins of his four horses, the block swings
beneath on its looped chain,
The Black man driving the long dray from the stone-yard, firm and
upright he stands balanced on one leg on the string-piece,
His blue shirt shows his broad neck and chest and falls loose over
his hip-band,
His look is tranquil and masterful, he brushes the droop of his hat
back from his brow,
The sun strikes his wiry hair and mustache, strikes the dark sheen of
his polished, flawless limbs.
source_text:
The negro holds firmly the reins of his four horses, the block swags
underneath on its tied-over chain,
The negro that drives the long dray of the stone-yard, steady and
tall he stands pois’d on one leg on the string-piece,
His blue shirt exposes his ample neck and breast and loosens over
his hip-band,
His glance is calm and commanding, he tosses the slouch of his hat
away from his forehead,
The sun falls on his crispy hair and mustache, falls on the black of
his polish’d and perfect limbs.

text: “I tell you,” said the man, clenching his hands and stamping with fury upon the floor, “I tell you I won’t have her laid in the ground. She could not rest there. The worms would plague her—not eat her—she is so wasted away.”
source_text: “I tell you,” said the man: clenching his hands, and stamping furiously on the floor,—“I tell you I won’t have her put into the ground. She couldn’t rest there. The worms would worry her—not eat her—she is so worn away.”

text:
O Gowdie, terror o’ the whigs,
Dread o’ blackcoats and rev’rend wigs!
Sour Bigotry, on her last legs,
Girns an’ looks back,
Wishing the ten Egyptian plagues
May seize you quick.

metadata.title: Poems and Songs of Robert Burns

text: “Hold, thou white-livered hound!” cried the robber, shouting after Toby Crackit, who, besting the matter with his long legs, was already in the lead. “Hold!”
source_text: “Stop, you white-livered hound!” cried the robber, shouting after Toby Crackit, who, making the best use of his long legs, was already ahead. “Stop!”

text: “Yes; but the worst of it is that, though Paul was admirable as a man in the Bible, he would scarcely have served in real life.”
source_text: “Yes; but the worst of it is that though Paul was excellent as a man in the Bible he would hardly have done in real life.”

text: By now she had risen; and the two of them drifted on together, without speaking, for two or three minutes, when Eustacia broke the silence by saying, “I suppose I must congratulate you.”
source_text: By this time she had risen; and they involuntarily sauntered on together, without speaking, for two or three minutes; when Eustacia broke silence by saying, “I assume I must congratulate you.”

text: Another line of research attempts to design specific encoder-decoder architectures for NMT models. For example, Britz et al. (2017) consider domain-aware embedding given by the encoder, and then jointly train a domain classifier, taking the embedding as input to incorporate the domain information. Zeng et al. (2018) ; Su et al. (2019) further extend their approach by separating the domainshared and domain-specific knowledge within the embedding. In addition, Zeng et al. (2018) and Shen et al. (2017) propose a maximum weighted likelihood estimation method, where the weight is obtained by word-level domain aware masking to encourage the model to pay more attention to the domain-specific words. The aforementioned methods, however, have a notable limitation: They enforce one single encoder to learn shared embedding across all domains, which often lacks adaptivity to each individual domain.

metadata.title: Multi-Domain Neural Machine Translation with Word-Level Adaptive Layer-wise Domain Mixing

text: “I must say, Mr. Eden,” the editor of _The Hornet_ called down, “that for a poet you can go some yourself. Where did you learn that right cross—if I may ask?”

Were stirred in him by some concealed decree,
That bade them burn, and live, and wheel away,
Like lights and voices, from haunted tower to tower
source_text:
Were driven within him by some secret power,
Which bade them blaze, and live, and roll afar,
Like lights and sounds, from haunted tower to tower

text: “If those eyes of yours were bed-winches,” Miss Pross replied, “and I were an English four-poster, you should not draw a splinter from me. No, you wicked foreign woman; I am your match.”
source_text: “If those eyes of yours were bed-winches,” returned Miss Pross, “and I was an English four-poster, they shouldn’t loose a splinter of me. No, you wicked foreign woman; I am your match.”

text:
I wondered which would miss me least,
And when Thanksgiving came,
If father’d put out more plates
To make the reckoning even.
source_text:
I wondered which would miss me least,
And when Thanksgiving came,
If father'd multiply the plates
To make an even sum.

text: Given an autoregressive language model that defines a probability distribution over the next token given the previous tokens in a sequence, a decoding strategy generates text by deciding how to output a token at each step based on the predicted distributions. Perhaps the most straightforward decoding strategy is to randomly choose a token with probability proportional to its likelihood. A challenge with the random sampling approach is that these probability distributions often contain a long tail of vocabulary items that are individually low-probability but cumulatively comprise a substantial amount of probability mass. Holtzman et al. (2020) observe that choosing tokens from this tail often leads to incoherent generations.

metadata.title: Automatic Detection of Generated Text is Easiest when Humans are Fooled

text: Understanding how people read and process text has long been a major topic in both cognitive science and natural language processing (NLP) research (Graesser et al., 1980; Liversedge et al., 1998; Frank et al., 2013a; Busjahn et al., 2014; Seppi, 2019, 2020). Online content providers and consumers are likewise interested in this line of work; in today’s increasingly busy world, readers often do not have time for lengthy articles, which leads content creators to target particular reading durations. Many providers have even analyzed traffic patterns to identify the ideal content length, with the general consensus suggesting that 3–7 minutes of work done as part of a capstone course with Adobe 1 Medium's study can be found here. content is optimal. Having determined the preferred content length, article writers then face the next challenge: when has a post reached that ideal length? A news article about last night’s football game may be easier to read than a technical post about NLP. Likewise, font type or size may affect a consumer’s comprehension and thereby slow the reading process. Many factors, both textual and stylistic, therefore immediately come to mind when considering the possible reading time of an article.
source_text: Understanding how we read and process text has proven a large area of both cognitive science and natural language processing (NLP) research (Graesser et al., 1980; Liversedge et al., 1998; Frank et al., 2013a; Busjahn et al., 2014; Seppi, 2019, 2020) . Online content providers and consumers are also interested in this research; in the increasingly busy world of today, consumers lack the time to read long articles, prompting content creators to aim for specific reading lengths. Many providers 1 have even examined traffic patterns in order to determine the ideal content length, with the general consensus finding 3-7 minutes of Work done as part of a capstone course with Adobe 1 Medium's study can be found here. content optimal. Thus, having established the optimal content length, article writers now face the next hurdle: when has their post reached the ideal length? A news article about last night's football game may be easier to read than a technical post about NLP. Perhaps the font type or size influences the consumer's comprehension, slowing down the reading process. There are many factors, both textual and stylistic, that quickly come to mind when considering the potential reading time of an article.

text:
What fortitude the soul contains,
That it can thus endure
The cadence of an approaching tread,
The opening of a door!
source_text:
What fortitude the soul contains,
That it can so endure
The accent of a coming foot,
The opening of a door!

text: “That is no excuse,” replied Mr. Brownlow. “You were there when these trinkets were destroyed, and, in the eye of the law, you are indeed the more culpable of the two; for the law assumes that your wife acts by your direction.”
source_text: “That is no excuse,” replied Mr. Brownlow. “You were present on the occasion of the destruction of these trinkets, and indeed are the more guilty of the two, in the eye of the law; for the law supposes that your wife acts under your direction.”

text:
STRANGER:
Warrior! I can ease thy woes,
Wilt thou, wilt thou, come with me—
Warrior! I can all disclose,
Follow, follow, follow me.

metadata.title: The Complete Poetical Works of Percy Bysshe Shelley — Complete

text: “Well, Miss—begging your pardon—I thought you might like them. But I know I can’t rival you in laying my thoughts out on my tongue. I was never much of a hand at what goes on inside me. Still, I thank you. Come, give me your hand.”
source_text: “Well, Miss—excuse the words—I thought you would like them. But I can’t match you, I know, in mapping out my mind upon my tongue. I never was very clever in my inside. But I thank you. Come, give me your hand.”

text: I, Clarence, must write it for him. He proposed that we two go out and see if any help could be accorded the wounded. I was strenuous against the project. I said that if there were many, we could do but little for them; and it would not be wise for us to trust ourselves among them, anyway. But he could seldom be turned from a purpose once formed; so we shut off the electric current from the fences, took an escort along, climbed over the enclosing ramparts of dead knights, and moved out upon the field. The first wounded man who appealed for help was sitting with his back against a dead comrade. When The Boss bent over him and spoke to him, the man recognized him and stabbed him. That knight was Sir Meliagraunce, as I found out by tearing off his helmet. He will not ask for help any more.

Cotopaxi! bid the sound
Through thy sister mountains ring,
Till each valley smile around
At the blissful welcoming!
And, O thou stern Ocean deep,
Thou whose foamy billows sweep
Shores where thousands wake to weep
Whilst they curse a villain king,
On the winds that fan thy breast
Bear thou news of Freedom’s rest!

metadata.title: The Complete Poetical Works of Percy Bysshe Shelley — Complete

text:
’Twas like an eye that seemed to smile on me.  
I gazed, until, by sunmade pallor waned, it sank  
Beneath the billows of the surging sea;  
Yet from its rays my spirit deep love drank,  
And to my brain the boundless world now shrank  
To one thought—one sole image—ay, for ever!  
Even as the dawn, on vapours dimly dank,  
The beams of that one Star did shoot and quiver  
Through my benighted mind—and were extinguished never.
source_text:
‘’Twas like an eye which seemed to smile on me.
I watched, till by the sun made pale, it sank
Under the billows of the heaving sea;
But from its beams deep love my spirit drank,
And to my brain the boundless world now shrank
Into one thought—one image—yes, for ever!
Even like the dayspring, poured on vapours dank,
The beams of that one Star did shoot and quiver
Through my benighted mind—and were extinguished never.

text:
Hail, string-empowerin’, clatterin’ Willie!  
Though fortune’s path be steep and stony hilly  
To every fiddling, rhyming billie,  
We pay no heed,  
But bear it like the unbroken filly,  
Proud in her speed.
source_text:
Hail, thairm-inspirin’, rattlin’ Willie!
Tho’ fortune’s road be rough an’ hilly
To every fiddling, rhyming billie,
We never heed,
But take it like the unback’d filly,
Proud o’ her speed.

text: “And must you go? Ah! there was a time, Frank, when it would have needed many promises to other people to tear you from my side. You were wont to call me darling then. But now it matters little to you how my days are spent.”
source_text: “And must you go? Ah! there was a time, Frank, when it would have taken a good many promises to other people to drag you away from me. You used to call me darling, then. But it doesn’t matter to you how my days are passed now.”

text:
Poet:
Fresh and rosy red the sun is mounting high,
On floats the sea in distant blue careering through its channels,
On floats the wind over the breast of the sea setting in toward land,
The great steady wind from west or west-by-south,
Floating so buoyant with milk-white foam on the waters.
“I didn’t have the carfare, and I live across the Bay,” Martin answered bluntly, with the idea of showing them his imperative need for the money.
“I won’t touch you at all—not even your hair. I am only going to kill that caterpillar settling on you. Now: still!”
